items.title: Classes, fields and methods: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A class is encapsulated as long as every field has a public getter and setter.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Keep representation private and expose behavior that preserves invariants; do not publish mutation merely to mirror each field.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Keep representation private and expose behavior that preserves invariants; do not publish mutation merely to mirror each field.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Keep representation private and expose behavior that preserves invariants; do not publish mutation merely to mirror each field.
A class is encapsulated as long as every field has a public getter and setter.

answer.explanation: Keep representation private and expose behavior that preserves invariants; do not publish mutation merely to mirror each field.

items.title: Classes, fields and methods: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read a non-negative starting count and number of increments. Implement a Counter with private state and print the final value.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Private representation is changed only through cohesive methods that preserve the non-negative invariant.
